rationale: In the citing paper, Seiler, Tuchman, and Yao (2021) appears only once in a grouped parenthetical citation listing examples of marketing work on public policy and consumer health. The paper does not discuss this soda-tax study's findings, mechanisms, data, or methods, and it is not used to build hypotheses, model structure, or interpretation. This indicates a broad background/literature-list use rather than a paper-specific functional role.
